game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **The Talos Principle**: This first-person philosophical puzzle game tasks players with navigating ancient ruins to solve over 120 intricate environmental puzzles. Movement is controlled via a virtual joystick on the left side of the screen, with camera control handled by swiping on the right, while tapping interacts with objects and picks up/places puzzle-solving tools. Each puzzle requires players to strategically deploy a limited set of tools, such as "jammers" to disable hostile drones and turrets, "connectors" to establish energy links, or "boxes" to reach elevated areas, often in a specific sequence to open gates or activate mechanisms. Successfully solving a puzzle awards a "Sigil" (a unique Tetromino-shaped piece) which is then used in a separate meta-puzzle to unlock new hub areas and further progression. Difficulty scales by introducing new tools, increasing the complexity of their interactions, and requiring multi-stage solutions across larger puzzle environments.
game_info.description: Navigate through ancient ruins solving intricate puzzles to collect Sigils. Use tools like Jammers to disable turrets, Connectors to create energy beams, and Boxes to reach elevated areas. Each puzzle requires strategic placement of limited tools to unlock gates and progress. Collect all Sigils to complete your journey through the ruins.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move player forward/backward and turn left/right
Space: Interact with objects (pick up/place tools, activate switches)
Z: Drop currently held tool
Shift: Sprint (move faster)
Enter: Start game
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and interaction testing. Validates that the player can move around the environment, pick up tools, and interact with basic puzzle elements without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the player character around the play area, attempt to pick up available tools, and interact with nearby objects. Test all movement directions and basic interactions to ensure responsive controls.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully moves in all directions, picks up and drops tools, and can interact with puzzle elements. No collision errors or movement glitches occur during basic gameplay.

TEST_2.test_description: Complete win condition testing. Tests the optimal strategy to solve all puzzles and collect all Sigils to achieve the win condition.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Navigate to each puzzle room in sequence, use tools strategically to disable turrets, create energy connections, and open gates. Collect each Sigil by solving progressively complex puzzles using the available tools efficiently.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: All puzzles are solved successfully, all Sigils are collected, and the game transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN state. The strategy demonstrates mastery of all puzzle mechanics and tool interactions.
